title: Master Hugues Of Saxe-Gotha
content:
آ آ آ آ Hist, but a word, fair and soft!
آ آ آ آ Forth and be judged, Master Hugues!
آ آ آ آ Answer the question Iï؟½ve put you so oft:
آ آ آ آ What do you mean by your mountainous fugues?
آ آ آ آ See, weï؟½re alone in the loft,

آ آ آ آ I, the poor organist here,
آ آ آ آ Hugues, the composer of note,
آ آ آ آ Dead through, and done with, this many a year:
آ آ آ آ Letï؟½s have a colloquy, something to quote,
آ آ آ آ Make the world prick up its ear!

آ آ آ آ See, the church empties apace:
آ آ آ آ Fast they extinguish the lights.
آ آ آ آ Hallo there, sacristan! Five minutesï؟½ grace!
آ آ آ آ Hereï؟½s a crank pedal wants setting to rights,
آ آ آ آ Baulks one of holding the base.

آ آ آ آ See, our huge house of the sounds,
آ آ آ آ Hushing its hundreds at once,
آ آ آ آ Bids the last loiterer back to his bounds!
آ آ آ آ O you may challenge them, not a response
آ آ آ آ Get the church-saints on their rounds!

آ آ آ آ (Saints go their rounds, who shall doubt?
آ آ آ آ March, with the moon to admire,
آ آ آ آ Up nave, down chancel, turn transept about,
آ آ آ آ Supervise all betwixt pavement and spire,
آ آ آ آ Put rats and mice to the rout

آ آ آ آ Aloys and Jurien and Just
آ آ آ آ Order things back to their place,
آ آ آ آ Have a sharp eye lest the candlesticks rust,
آ آ آ آ Rub the church-plate, darn the sacrament-lace,
آ آ آ آ Clear the desk-velvet of dust.)

آ آ آ آ Hereï؟½s you book, younger folks shelve!
آ آ آ آ Played I not off-hand and runningly,
آ آ آ آ Just now, your masterpiece, hard number twelve?
آ آ آ آ Hereï؟½s what should strike, could one handle it cunningly:
آ آ آ آ Help the axe, give it a helve!

آ آ آ آ Page after page as I played,
آ آ آ آ Every barï؟½s rest, where one wipes
آ آ آ آ Sweat from oneï؟½s brow, I looked up and surveyed,
آ آ آ آ Oï؟½er my three claviers, yon forest of pipes
آ آ آ آ Whence you still peeped in the shade.

آ آ آ آ Sure you were wishful to speak,
آ آ آ آ You, with brow ruled like a score,
آ آ آ آ Yes, and eyes buried in pits on each cheek,
آ آ آ آ Like two great breves, as they wrote them of yore,
آ آ آ آ Each side that bar, your straight beak!

آ آ آ آ Sure you said ï؟½Good, the mere notes!
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Still, couldst thou take my intent,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Know what procured me our Companyï؟½s votes
آ آ آ آ ï؟½A master were lauded and sciolists shent,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Parted the sheep from the goats!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Well then, speak up, never flinch!
آ آ آ آ Quick, ere my candleï؟½s a snuff
آ آ آ آ Burnt, do you see? to its uttermost inch
آ آ آ آ I believe in you, but thatï؟½s not enough:
آ آ آ آ Give my conviction a clinch!

آ آ آ آ First you deliver your phrase
آ آ آ آ Nothing propound, that I see,
آ آ آ آ Fit in itself for much blame or much praise
آ آ آ آ Answered no less, where no answer needs be:
آ آ آ آ Off start the Two on their ways.

آ آ آ آ Straight must a Third interpose,
آ آ آ آ Volunteer needlessly help;
آ آ آ آ In strikes a Fourth, a Fifth thrusts in his nose,
آ آ آ آ So the cryï؟½s open, the kennelï؟½s a-yelp,
آ آ آ آ Argumentï؟½s hot to the close.

آ آ آ آ One dissertates, he is candid;
آ آ آ آ Two must discept, has distinguished;
آ آ آ آ Three helps the couple, if ever yet man did;
آ آ آ آ Four protests; Five makes a dart at the thing wished:
آ آ آ آ Back to One, goes the case bandied.

آ آ آ آ One says his say with a difference;
آ آ آ آ More of expounding, explaining!
آ آ آ آ All now is wrangle, abuse and vociferance;
آ آ آ آ Now thereï؟½s a truce, allï؟½s subdued, self-restraining;
آ آ آ آ Five, though, stands out all the stiffer hence.

آ آ آ آ One is incisive, corrosive;
آ آ آ آ Two retorts, nettled, curt, crepitant;
آ آ آ آ Three makes rejoinder, expansive, explosive;
آ آ آ آ Four overbears them all, strident and strepitant:
آ آ آ آ Five. . . O Danaides, O Sieve!

آ آ آ آ Now, they ply axes and crowbars;
آ آ آ آ Now, they prick pins at a tissue
آ آ آ آ Fine as a skein of the casuist Escobarï؟½s
آ آ آ آ Worked on the bone of a lie. To what issue?
آ آ آ آ Where is our gain at the two-bars?

آ آ آ آ Est fuga, volitur rota.
آ آ آ آ One we drift: where looms the dim port?
آ آ آ آ One, Two, Three, Four, Five, contribute their quota;
آ آ آ آ Something is gained, if one caught but the import
آ آ آ آ Show it us, Hugues of Saxe-Gotha!

آ آ آ آ What with affirming, denying,
آ آ آ آ Holding, risposting, subjoining,
آ آ آ آ Allï؟½s like. . . itï؟½s like. . . for an instant Iï؟½m trying. . .
آ آ آ آ There! See our roof, its gilt moulding and groining
آ آ آ آ Under those spider-webs lying!

آ آ آ آ So your fugue broadens and thickens,
آ آ آ آ Greatens and deepens and lengthens,
آ آ آ آ Till we exclaim ï؟½But whereï؟½s the music, the dickens?
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Blot ye the gold, while your spider-web strengthens
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Blacked to the stoutest of tickens?ï؟½


آ آ آ آ I for manï؟½s effort am zealous:
آ آ آ آ Prove me such censure unfounded!
آ آ آ آ Seems it surprising a lover grows jealous -
آ آ آ آ Hopes ï؟½t was for something, his organ-pipes sounded,
آ آ آ آ Tiring three boys at the bellows?

آ آ آ آ Is it your moral of life?
آ آ آ آ Such a web, simple and subtle,
آ آ آ آ Weave we in earth here in impotent strife,
آ آ آ آ Backward and forward each throwing his shuttle,
آ آ آ آ Death ending all with a knife?

آ آ آ آ Over our heads truth and nature
آ آ آ آ Still our lifeï؟½s zigzags and dodges,
آ آ آ آ Ins and outs, weaving a new legislature
آ آ آ آ Godï؟½s gold just shining its last where that lodges,
آ آ آ آ Palled beneath manï؟½s usurpature.

آ آ آ آ So we oï؟½ershroud stars and roses,
آ آ آ آ Cherub and trophy and garland;
آ آ آ آ Nothings grow something which quietly closes
آ آ آ آ Heavenï؟½s earnest eye: not a glimpse of the far land
آ آ آ آ Gets through our comments and glozes.

آ آ آ آ Ah but traditions, inventions,
آ آ آ آ (Say we and make up a visage)
آ آ آ آ So many men with such various intentions,
آ آ آ آ Down the past ages, must know more than this age!
آ آ آ آ Leave we the web its dimensions!

آ آ آ آ Who thinks Hugues wrote for the deaf,
آ آ آ آ Proved a mere mountain in labour?
آ آ آ آ Better submit; try again; whatï؟½s the clef?
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Faith, ï؟½t is no trifle for pipe and for tabor
آ آ آ آ Four flats, the minor in F.

آ آ آ آ Friend, your fugue taxes the finger:
آ آ آ آ Learning it once, who would lose it?
آ آ آ آ Yet all the while a misgiving will linger,
آ آ آ آ Truthï؟½s golden oï؟½er us although we refuse it
آ آ آ آ Nature, throï؟½ cobwebs we string her.

آ آ آ آ Hugues! I advise meï؟½ poenï؟½
آ آ آ آ (Counterpoint glares like a Gorgon)
آ آ آ آ Bid One, Two, Three, Four, Five, clear the arena!
آ آ آ آ Say the word, straight I unstop the full-organ,
آ آ آ آ Blare out the mode Palestrina.

آ آ آ آ While in the roof, if Iï؟½m right there,
آ آ آ آ . . . Lo you, the wick in the socket!
آ آ آ آ Hallo, you sacristan, show us a light there!
آ آ آ آ Down it dips, gone like a rocket.
آ آ آ آ What, you want, do you, to come unawares,
آ آ آ آ Sweeping the church up for first morning-prayers,
آ آ آ آ And find a poor devil has ended his cares
آ آ آ آ At the foot of your rotten-runged rat-riddled stairs?
آ آ آ آ Do I carry the moon in my pocket?
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Browning, titled "Master Hugues Of Saxe-Gotha", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
